questions.question: Give two examples each, of modes of transport used on land, water and air.

solution_data.solution:
1. Land - Bus, truck, train.
  2. Water - Ship, boat.
  3. Air - Aeroplane, Helicopter.

questions.question: Motion of a child on a swing is ________.
Motion of the needle of a sewing machine is ________.
Motion of the wheel of a bicycle is ________.
Why can a pace or a footstep not be used as a standard unit of length ?

solution_data.solution: Pace or footstep as a unit are not suitable. As their measure is not fixed and varies from person to person. Since a standard unit must be similar in all places of world and in all weathers. It should not depend upon body parts and natural phenomenon.

questions.question: Arrange the following length into their increasing magnitude :1 metre, 1 centimetre, 1 kilometre, 1 millimetre
The height of a person is 1.65 m. Express it into cm and mm.

solution_data.solution:
Given that, height of person = 1.65 m
We have to find out
(a) height in cm
(b) height in mm
(a) We know that,
1 m = 100 cm
Hence, 1.65 m = 1.65 × 100 cm
= 165 cm
(b) Again we know that,
1 m = 1000 mm
Hence, 1.65 m = 1.65 × 1000 mm
= 1650 mm.

questions.question: The distance between Radha's home and her school is 3250 m. Express this distance in kilometres.

solution_data.solution:
Given that,
Distance between Radha's home and her school = 3250 m
We have to find out distance in km.
We know that,
1000 m = 1 km
Hence, 3250 m {tex}=\frac{3250}{1000}{/tex}  km = 3.250 km.

questions.question: While measuring the length of a knitting needle, the reading of the scale at one end is 3.0 cm and at the other end is 33.1 cm. What is the length of the needle?

solution_data.solution:
Given that,
Reading at starting end = 3.0 cm
Reading at other end = 33.1 cm
We know that,
Length of the object = (Reading at other end – Reading at starting end)
Hence Length of the needle = 33.1 – 3.0 cm
= 30.1 cm.

questions.question: Write the similarities and differences between the motion of a bicycle and a ceiling fan that has been switched on.

solution_data.solution:
(i) Similarity: Both the wheel of a bicycle and a ceiling fan execute a motion
(ii) Dissimilarity: A bicycle also executes a rectilinear motion while a fan does not execute such motion.

questions.question: Why would you not like to use a measuring tape made of an elastic material like rubber to measure distance? What would be some of the problems you would meet in telling someone about a distance you measured with such a tape?

solution_data.solution: Elastic substances have no fixed length. Their length is altered when they are stretched by different forces. Hence, an elastic tape will not give a correct measure. This is why we can not use an elastic measuring tape. As it will always give a reading greater than the actual one and also since it is very much difficult to measure the amount by which it alters the measure we can not tell correctly a distance by measuring with an elastic tape.

Two examples of periodic motion are :

  1. Motion of earth round the sun.
  2. Oscillations of a pendulum.
